meaning: A system formed by the interaction of living organisms with their physical environment.
A large-scale ecosystem defined by its climate, vegetation and animal life.
The variety of plant and animal life in a particular habitat or on Earth as a whole.
An organism that creates its own food through photosynthesis, typically a plant.
An organism that obtains energy by feeding on other organisms.
A sequence showing the transfer of energy from one organism to the next.
The movement and exchange of nutrients between the environment and living organisms.
The large-scale removal of forest, often to clear land for farming or urban development.
A dense forest found near the equator with high rainfall and biodiversity.
The dense upper layer of a tropical rainforest formed by the tops of tall trees.
The washing of nutrients down through the soil by heavy rainfall.
The way in which organisms and their environment depend on and affect each other.
A biome receiving less than 250mm of rainfall per year with extreme daytime temperatures.
Features of an organism that help it survive in its particular environment.
The process by which fertile land becomes desert, typically as a result of drought or poor land management.
Using natural resources in a way that meets current needs without harming future generations.
Tourism that is designed to support conservation and benefit local communities.
Groups of people who are the original inhabitants of an area and have strong ties to their land.
A natural environment, such as a forest or ocean, that absorbs more carbon than it releases.
The process by which water is absorbed by plants and then released as water vapour from leaves.
